sizing_rule.note: Reduce position size proportionally when trading multiple correlated systems simultaneously. Position size = base_size / num_systems. Example: trading 10 systems with perfect correlation reduces individual position to 1/10 of single-system size. Uses FAHPR/FGHPR framework with fractional f (e.g., 0.5 × optimal_f) to control correlation drawdown risk.

source_rule_text: Position size should be reduced proportionally when multiple market systems are traded simultaneously to account for correlation risk, as simultaneous losses across systems could be terminal to the account.
